title: NA-Non ULIP
word_text:
Guide To Life Insurance Payout Options
A life insurance policy covers premature deaths and pays the benefit if the insured passes away during the coverage tenure. However, there’s more to a life insurance policy than just death benefits. Many plans offer a payout if the plan matures and the insured survives.
Money-back plans, on the other hand, offer periodic payments called survival benefits while pension plans pay annuity incomes, lifelong.
So, there are different pay-out options in life insurance plans. Here’s a complete guide to such payouts.
How Does A Life Insurance Policy Pay-out Work in India?
In India, life insurance plans pay a benefit in one or more of the following instances –
If the insured dies
If the insured survives till maturity and there’s a provision for maturity benefit
Survival benefits at regular intervals in money-back plans
Settlement option in ULIPs wherein the maturity benefit is paid in instalments over five years
Pension payments in annuity plans
Commutation benefit under deferred pension plans
Rider benefit payment if applicable
Under these events, you file a claim with the insurer and the insurer makes the corresponding pay-out.
How do you file a claim with the insurance company?
The filing of a claim depends on the type of claim that is incurred. Have a look –
Death claim
In a death claim, the nominee has to inform the insurer of the insured’s death. The claim form and relevant documents should be submitted. The insurer verifies the documents and settles the claims.
Maturity and money-back claims
The policyholder fills out and submits a discharge voucher sent by the insurer days before the maturity date. Relevant documents are also required with the form. After the documentation is done, the insurer pays the maturity benefit after the maturity date.
Rider claims
The insurer should be notified. The claim form and documents should be submitted. The insurer verifies the authenticity of the claim and makes the payment.
What are the documents needed to obtain death benefits2?
In the case of death, the following documents are usually required for the claim –
Claim form, filled and signed by the nominee.
Original policy bond.
Death certificate of the insured.
Police FIR, panchnama, coroner’s report, inquest report, etc. in the case of accidental deaths
Assignment or re-assignment deed, if any.
Medical reports in the case of death due to an accident.
Succession certificate or any other legal evidence if proper nomination is not done.
Identity proof of the nominee.
Bank account details of the nominee.
Any other document required by the insurer.
What are the formalities involved in obtaining maturity benefits?
Maturity benefits are quite easily paid since you survive the tenure and the coverage is near expiration. The insurance company starts preparing the maturity payout in advance. A discharge voucher is usually sent to the policyholder. You have to fill and submit the form. The insurer might ask you for a copy of the insurance policy bond, your bank details and identity proof if needed. Once the documentation is complete, the maturity benefit is paid.
What are the different pay-out options available1?
In India, life insurance pay-outs can be in the following manner –
Lump sum pay-out:
This is the simplest mode of pay-out in insurance wherein the benefit is paid in a lump sum.
Instalment pay-out:
Under the instalment pay-out option, the benefit is paid in instalments. These instalments can be paid monthly, quarterly, half-yearly or annually. The frequency of payment can be determined by the insurer and stated in the policy document. Alternatively, you might have the choice of selecting the frequency yourself depending on your requirements.
The duration of benefit payment also depends on the plan. Some plans might specify a maximum tenure up to which the payments would be made while some plans might pay the benefit lifelong. A very common example is a pension plan wherein the annuity payments are paid for as long as you are alive. Moreover, if you choose the joint life annuity option, the payments continue till either you or your spouse are alive.
The instalment amount might also be different. Some plans pay a uniform income over the specified tenure while others offer increasing instalments.
Lump sum + instalment pay-out:
This pay-out in insurance is a combination of both the aforementioned pay-out options. Under this mode, a part of the benefit is paid in a lump sum while the remaining is paid in instalments. In the case of instalment payments, the frequency, duration and amount of instalment are similar to the instalment pay-out option.
How long does it take for a life insurance payout to be made?
The time for the payment of the benefit depends on the insurer and the type of claim that you raise. In some cases, the pay-out can be done within a few days while in other cases it might take some time. So, check your insurer’s TAT (Turn-Around-Time) on claim payments.
Conclusion:
Life insurance plans have different pay-out options and modes. So, when buying a policy, check the benefit pay-out structure. Ensure that you buy the right plan that pays the benefits when you need them.
